h2: What to include in your application
h3: Reasons you might not be eligible to apply

sections.heading: On this page
bullets: What to include in your application

sections.heading: What is an ECA
items: If you have a completed foreign educational credential, like a high school diploma (or higher), you must get an ECA from an approved organization.
An ECA is used to check that your foreign credential is valid and equal to a Canadian secondary school diploma or higher.

sections.heading: Choose a designated organization
items: You can get your assessment from any of these organizations:
Check the websites of the approved organizations or contact them directly to find out
bullets: how long an assessment will take

sections.heading: Apply for your assessment
bullets: apply for an ECA for immigration purposes
gather all the documents the organization needs to assess your education
ask your school for multiple copies of your degrees, diplomas, transcripts, etc.
follow any instructions given to submit the documents

sections.heading: What your report means
items: Once you get your education assessed, the organization will give you an ECA report that will tell you if your foreign credentials are equal to a Canadian secondary school diploma or higher.
bullets: clearly state that your completed foreign credential is equal to a completed Canadian secondary school diploma or a higher-level diploma
be issued on or after the date the organization was approved by us
be less than 5 years old on the date that we receive your application

sections.heading: Reasons you might not be eligible to apply
items: You’re not eligible to apply if your report shows your completed foreign credential is equivalent to
bullets: a high school diploma from a non-recognized institution
incomplete years of studies (instead of a completed high school diploma or a higher-level diploma)

sections.heading: What to include in your application
items: In your application, you must include
Keep a copy of the report for your records and future use.
We’ll check any ECA report submitted with an application and with the organization that produced the report.
bullets: a copy of the ECA report
copies of your degrees, diplomas, transcripts or other proof of your completed foreign credential
